title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Sharp questions Colin McNaughton regarding his relationship with Celia Austin and her psychological state. McNaughton attributes her behavior to an unfortunate childhood and admits to a subconscious attraction to her, though he notes he does not intend to marry her soon due to financial instability. He believes Celia was well-liked and suggests her death may not have been motivated by personal grudges.

Sally Finch expresses fear regarding the atmosphere of the house and describes Mrs. Nikolai as a "cruel" figure. She suggests that Mrs. Silvia may have been killed because she intended to reveal truths about someone. Sally provides a critical observation: she saw Celia leaving the house through the front door around 10:00 PM to meet someone, despite Celia having previously stated she was going to bed. Sally notes that while the male servant, Jeronimo, locks the doors at 11:00 PM, anyone with a key could have entered the house before then.

Elizabeth Johnston is also questioned. She doubts that Celia destroyed her academic paper, arguing that Nigel Chapman is too intelligent to use his own ink, and suggests that Westlake might know the culprit. Elizabeth reveals that on the night of her death, Celia mentioned she had not taken down a light bulb and expressed suspicion that someone’s passport might be forged. This mention of a forged passport prompts Sharp to reflect on past police records involving foreign students at Nutmeg Lane, including a deported West African student and a search for a mixed-race man connected to a murder.
